item.title: 用 AI Prompt 的方式来记笔记 - I'm TualatriX

Save essay History of ideas Aristotle on making babies He was the first great observer of nature. But his theory of human reproduction was deeply sexist – and enduring Emily Thomas - Aeon

Save video History of technology Who owns history? How remarkable historical footage is hidden and monetised 18 minutes - Aeon

Save essay Human evolution How like the kiwi we are To understand helpless human babies, our big brains and oddly involved dads, look to the evolution of birds not mammals Antone Martinho-Truswell - Aeon
Save video Meaning and the good life Why Aristotle believed that philosophy was humanity’s highest purpose 9 minutes - Aeon
Save essay History of science Machina mundi How medieval thinkers foreshadowed modern physics in investigating the character of machines, devices and forces Henrik Lagerlund & Sylvain Roudaut - Aeon
Save video Space exploration What are you really seeing when you see magnificent images of space? 5 minutes - Aeon

Save essay Consciousness and altered states Animal, vegetable, mineral Cruel and unscientific, the ‘vegetative state’ diagnosis stems from a hierarchical and bigoted view of all living things Ben Platts-Mills - Aeon
Save video Politics and government Join the spirited debate at a women’s hair salon before a pivotal election in Tunisia 19 minutes - Aeon
Save essay The ancient world Guide to a foreign past The iconoclastic French historian Paul Veyne illuminated the past by showing how deeply alien it is to the present Carlos Noreña - Aeon
Save video Philosophy of mind An enigmatic ‘story of consciousness’ told through 19th-century engravings 7 minutes - Aeon
Save video Information and communication Mapping data visualisation’s meteoric rise from Victorian London to today 6 minutes - Aeon

Save essay Virtues and vices The virtue of discretion When the rules break down, you must judge what to do on your own. Discretion is necessary for navigating the muddle of life Lorraine Daston - Aeon
Save video Ecology and environmental sciences In an ancient English rainforest, John creates charcoal and cultivates growth 12 minutes - Aeon
Save video Philosophy of mind We may never settle the ‘free will’ debate, but tapping into it is still worthwhile 32 minutes - Aeon
Save essay Mood and emotion In praise of irritation Unlike anger, irritation has neither glamour nor radicalism on its side. Yet it might just be the mood we need right now Will Rees - Aeon
Save video Nations and empires The strange tale of how mangoes became hallowed objects in Maoist China 6 minutes - Aeon
Save essay History of ideas Disorient yourself Now associated with childhood fun, the swing has a near-universal history of ritual transgression and transformation Javier Moscoso - Aeon
Save essay Biology Octopus time We humans are forward-facing, gravity-bound plodders. Can the liquid motion of the octopus radicalise our ideas about time? David Borkenhagen - Aeon
Save essay Beauty and aesthetics Patterns of the lifeworld Machine learning theory is shedding new light on how to think about the mysterious and ineffable nature of art Peli Grietzer - Aeon
Save essay Anthropology Memories within myth The stories of oral societies, passed from generation to generation, are more than they seem. They are scientific records Patrick Nunn - Aeon

Save essay Family life Honey, I sold the kids We have laws to protect children from factory work. Why aren’t they protected from parents who monetise their lives online? Clarissa Sebag-Montefiore - Aeon
Save essay Quantum theory All is One The ancient philosophy of monism and the physics of quantum entanglement agree: all that exists is one unified whole Heinrich Päs - Aeon
